summary:
The primary limitation of intraoperative histopathology is its potential lack of full accuracy due to fixative effects; it aids mainly in determining resection margins and local tumor characteristics. Sentinel lymph node biopsies serve different diagnostic roles focusing on pre-surgical assessment instead of offering immediate surgical pathology results. \boxed{D}

The summary captures that while intraoperative histopathology has significant utility with notable limitations affecting precision, sentinel lymph node biopsies have a distinct purpose unrelated to delivering same-day pathological confirmation at surgery site. This aligns closely with Agent2’s original response by highlighting these critical distinctions within two concise statements as requested.
